checks: resolve the dispatch-boundary event for the exact child Permit
require the root, authority, child, and governed request liveness snapshot to be inside the dispatch-ownership transaction
require root authority not closed, revoked, expired, or replaced at the boundary
require child allow decision and required approval satisfied at the boundary
require current execution Policy and the non-bypassable safety floor to permit dispatch
body.ordering_rule: dispatch-start committed first means later revocation cannot claim prevention; close or revoke committed first means dispatch is unauthorized
non_claims: does not prove absence of an unrecorded provider call
does not establish provider outcome, job completion, or settlement

verdicts.supported: recorded dispatch-boundary evidence shows every required ancestor and execution gate live
verdicts.disproved: recorded evidence shows a required gate not live or an ordering conflict
verdicts.insufficient_evidence: the dispatch-boundary or required liveness/Policy evidence is absent
verdicts.unverifiable_scope: the boundary, liveness, or Policy-snapshot version is unsupported
